prompt: A CleanWave Offline Audio Prep tool that helps podcast editors, short‑form video teams, and course creators clean and package large batches of recordings entirely in the browser, without uploading media. On first launch, the user creates a local profile (username plus a device-only passphrase that must be at least 8 characters and include at least 1 number); this is a convenience gate, not secure authentication, and a “Reset this device profile” action clears the local profile and data. The main workspace is an English-language Svelte + TypeScript SPA with a Sidebar for Projects and Playlists, Tabs for Edit/Export/Reports, and a waveform Timeline editor (Canvas/SVG) with transport controls (play/pause, ±15s seek, and 0.5×–2.0× speed). Users can drag-and-drop import up to 200 audio files per batch with a combined size limit of 2 GB; only mp3/wav/ogg are accepted and invalid files are rejected with row-level errors in a Table. Editing supports cut/split/merge, fade in/out from 0.1s to 10.0s with an effect preview toggle, silence detection that flags regions below −35 dB lasting at least 0.6s, loudness normalization to −14 LUFS, and left/right balance adjustment (−100 to +100). Each project allows up to 50 markers with timestamped notes, and exports support mp3 (128/192/320 kbps) or wav (44.1 kHz), including batch export of up to 20 items via an “Export Cart” Drawer that can split jobs by format/bitrate and shows a local size/time estimate before confirmation. An offline playlist holds up to 1,000 tracks with search by filename or note, and playback modes include sequential, single-repeat, and shuffle; all confirmations, warnings, and progress are shown as in-app Toast messages and non-blocking Modals. The app runs fully offline with no backend and no network dependencies. Lightweight preferences (theme, playback speed, last-opened project, UI role selection) are stored in LocalStorage, while projects, markers, version snapshots, playlists, and imported audio blobs are stored in IndexedDB; auto-save runs every 30 seconds while the app is open and keeps the last 3 recoverable versions per project after accidental close/crash. Heavy operations (waveform generation, silence scanning, LUFS normalization, and export rendering) are dispatched to a Web Worker pool using a “smart dispatch” queue that prioritizes shorter tasks, respects user-defined “quiet hours” availability windows, tracks per-worker success rate as a local “rating,” and reclaims stalled tasks if runtime exceeds 2× the initial estimate. Multi-tab coordination uses BroadcastChannel to warn about concurrent edits and to offer “Open read-only” when the same project is active elsewhere. Local analytics dashboards and operations reports summarize imports→edits→exports conversion, processing time, and export formats with date filters and CSV export, with UI-level role views (Editor/Reviewer/Operations) that simply adjust visible menus. For optional integrations, an offline “Open Platform Kit” generates REST/GraphQL-style sample payloads and webhook-like event JSON into a user-chosen folder (File System Access API) with per-token daily quotas enforced locally; a cohort/organization module supports bulk CSV import/export and validation for schools/majors/classes/companies/positions and cohort time windows; and a face-recognition attendance engine can run real-time camera check-in or batch matching using a bundled on-device model with configurable Top‑N and confidence thresholds plus manual fallback.
